name: EventStoreDB Event Sourcing API
what_it_does: Purpose-built event sourcing database with gRPC and HTTP API — enabling developers to implement event sourcing and CQRS (Command Query Responsibility Segregation) patterns with immutable append-only event streams, server-side projections for event transformation, persistent subscriptions for event processing, and strong consistency guarantees.
use_cases: Implementing event sourcing via EventStoreDB API for immutable audit log and state reconstruction
Building CQRS systems via EventStoreDB for command/query separation with event streams
Creating event-driven architectures via EventStoreDB subscriptions for real-time event processing
Projecting events via EventStoreDB for materialized view computation from event streams
Replaying event history via EventStoreDB for point-in-time state reconstruction
not_for: General-purpose document or relational storage (EventStoreDB is event-specific)
High-throughput pub/sub at internet scale (use Kafka for massive fan-out streaming)
Teams unfamiliar with event sourcing patterns (requires event sourcing design expertise)
best_when: A development team is implementing event sourcing + CQRS architecture and needs a purpose-built database with native append-only streams, projections, and strong consistency — rather than adapting PostgreSQL or Kafka for event sourcing.
avoid_when: You're not implementing event sourcing, need general-purpose database capabilities, or want a fully managed cloud service without self-hosting.

auth.notes: HTTP Basic auth (username:password). Default admin: admin:changeit — MUST CHANGE in production. gRPC: also uses HTTP Basic or client certificate via TLS. REST HTTP API: legacy (still supported in v23). gRPC: preferred for new clients (EventStoreDB v20+). Client SDKs: official C#, Java, Python, Node.js, Go, Rust — use gRPC internally. Cluster auth: node-to-node gossip authentication via certificate. EventStoreDB Cloud: cloud-hosted with certificate-based auth.

pricing.paid_tiers: EventStoreDB Cloud: from $0.30/hr; Enterprise support: custom
pricing.notes: UK company (London). Founded 2013 by Greg Young (DDD/CQRS/event sourcing pioneer). Event Sourcing Architecture: immutable append-only event log. Streams: like tables in relational — append events, read by position. Projections: server-side JavaScript for event transformation (Built-in: $by_category, $by_event_type). Persistent subscriptions: durable consumer groups (like Kafka consumer groups). Catch-up subscriptions: read from any position, then live. Optimistic concurrency: expectedRevision for conflict detection. Licensing: EventStoreDB Community is free (source available, not OSI approved for v22+). EventStoreDB Cloud: managed. Used by: financial services, healthcare, any DDD/event sourcing teams.

performance.rate_limits: Disk I/O bound; single node: ~50K events/sec write; cluster: higher

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: gRPC preferred (not REST for new clients). HTTP Basic auth. DEFAULT admin:changeit MUST BE CHANGED. Official SDKs in 6 languages. expectedRevision for optimistic concurrency. Projections for event transformation. UK company. Community edition source-available (not OSI approved v22+).
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Reads idempotent by stream name and revision. Writes: use expectedRevision for optimistic concurrency (idempotent with same expected version). Append: not natively idempotent — implement idempotency via event ID deduplication at application layer.
known_agent_gotchas: DEFAULT CREDENTIALS: admin/changeit — MUST change in production before any network exposure
gRPC is the preferred protocol for new clients (v20+); HTTP REST API is legacy
expectedRevision: required for optimistic concurrency control — specify expected stream revision on writes
License change (v22+): EventStoreDB Community is source-available (not OSI open-source) — verify license
Projections: server-side JavaScript projections require careful design — excessive projections impact performance
Persistent vs catch-up subscriptions: persistent = server-managed checkpoints; catch-up = client-managed

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced (gRPC + HTTPS). HTTP Basic auth — CRITICAL: change default admin:changeit immediately. GDPR: event sourcing stores all events permanently — implement GDPR right-to-be-forgotten via encrypted event wrapping. Certificate-based node-to-node auth in clusters. UK company: GDPR compliance. Source-available (not OSI): auditability somewhat limited vs pure open-source. EventStoreDB Cloud: SOC2 certified. Self-hosted: operator responsible for certificate management.
